source: Liu, M. et al. — "SmartShot: Hunt Hidden Vulnerabilities in Smart Contracts using Mutable Snapshots", FSE 2025 (Proc. ACM Softw. Eng., Vol. 2, FSE).
methodology_note: SmartShot is a snapshot fuzzer that targets broad DeFi vulnerability classes (reentrancy, access control, arithmetic, etc.) on a corpus of 42,738 real-world Ethereum smart contracts. Per the published abstract it identifies 2,150 vulnerable contracts (2.1×-13.7× more than ItyFuzz/Smartian) and 24 0-days in the latest 10,000 contracts. Its evaluation does not target the specific cross-chain bridge incidents in our 12-benchmark set — vulnerability classes are mismatched (DeFi protocol bugs vs cross-chain semantic invariants). Cells therefore stay null with a domain-mismatch note. The aggregate fuzzing-speedup figures are recorded separately in case they are useful as a sanity reference for the BridgeSentry vs ItyFuzz comparison.

polynetwork.note: domain mismatch — SmartShot eval set is general DeFi contracts, not cross-chain bridge incidents

_instructions_for_filling: Filled 2026-04-28 from the FSE 2025 program page + ACM DOI 10.1145/3715714. SmartShot does not enumerate per-bridge results because its evaluation domain is general DeFi vulnerability discovery on 42,738 contracts — not the cross-chain bridge incident set we benchmark. Per-bridge cells therefore stay null with a 'domain mismatch' note. Update if a SmartShot follow-up evaluates on individual bridge incidents.
